title: Apparatuses and methods for compute enabled cache
abstract: The present disclosure includes apparatuses and methods for compute enabled cache. An example apparatus comprises a compute component, a memory and a controller coupled to the memory. The controller configured to operate on a block select and a subrow select as metadata to a cache line to control placement of the cache line in the memory to allow for a compute enabled cache.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus, comprising: a memory, configured to store cache data, having a compute component; and a controller coupled to the cache memory, the controller configured to: create a block select as metadata to a cache line; and create a subrow select as metadata to the cache line. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the controller is a cache controller and the memory is a last layer cache (LLC) memory. 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the compute component is configured to access and to operate on cached data in the LLC memory without moving the cached data to a higher level in cache memory. 4. The apparatus of any of claims 1-3, wherein the compute component is a static random access memory (SRAM) logic resource integrated with a memory array of the memory. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein: the block select enables an offset to the cache line; and the subrow select enables multiple sets to a set associative cache. 6. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the block select provides an offset to a page in a dynamic random access memory (DRAM) page. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the block select and the subrow select are not part of an address space of a host processor. 8. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the controller is configured to: change the block select and the subrow select; and relocate the cached data transparently to the host processor. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the subrow select controls placement of a cache line on multiple different rows in an array in the memory. 10. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the controller is configured to: use the block select to control alignment of cached data in an array in the memory; and use the subrow select to control resource allocation in the array. 11. The apparatus of any of claims 5-10, wherein the cache controller is configured to: use the block select to choose which part of a row in the array having a first bit width to access as a chunk having a second bit width as part of an array access request; and use the block select and the subrow select in a repeated manner to allow a cache line to be split and placed differently in a bank of dynamic random access memory (DRAM). 12. An apparatus, comprising: a memory device configured to couple to a host, wherein the memory device includes: an array of memory cells; sensing circuitry coupled to the array, the sensing circuitry including a sense amplifier and a compute component configured to perform logical operations; and a controller coupled to the array and sensing circuitry, the controller configured to: receive a cache line having block select metadata and subrow select metadata; and operate on the block select metadata and subrow select metadata to: control alignment of cache blocks in the array; and allow the cache line to be placed on a particular row of the array. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the apparatus further includes a cache controller to: create the block select metadata and insert to the cache line); and create the subrow select metadata and insert to the cache line. 14. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the controller is configured to: operate on the cache line to control alignment of cache blocks in the array according to the block select metadata; and operate on the cache line to allow the cache line to be placed on multiple different rows to the array according to the row select metadata. 15. The apparatus of any of claims 12-14, wherein: each cache line is handled as multiple vectors of different bit length values; and the controller is configured to handle each vector of the multiple vectors as a chunk. 16. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the apparatus further comprises: a processing resource; a memory having the array and coupled to the processing resource; and an interface between the processing resource and the memory. 17. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the interface comprises: through silicon vias (TS Vs) coupling the processing resource to the memory; and the apparatus is a 3D integrated logic/memory. 18. The apparatus of any of claims 16-18, wherein the processing resource comprises a static random access memory (SRAM) and the controller is configured to store the block select and the subrow select in the SRAM. 19. The apparatus of any of claims 16-18, wherein the memory is a processing in memory (PEVI) device, the array is a dynamic random access memory (DRAM) array, and wherein the PEVI device includes: 

 sensing circuitry coupled to the array, the sensing circuitry including a sense amplifier and a compute component configured to perform logical operations; and wherein the controller is coupled to the array and the sensing circuitry, the controller configured to: receive the cache line having block select metadata and subrow select metadata from the processing resource; and operate on the block select metadata and subrow select metadata to: control alignment of a cache block in the array; and control placement of the cache line on a particular row of the array. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, wherein the controller is configured to: operate on the cache line to control alignment of the cache block in the array according to the block select metadata; and operate on the cache line to allow the cache line to be placed on multiple different rows to the array according to the row select metadata. 21. The apparatus of claim 19, wherein each cache line is handled as multiple vectors, the cache block is a vector, the vector is a plurality of defined bit length values, and wherein each of the plurality of defined bit length values is an element usable in a logical operation. 22. A method for operating a cache memory, comprising: creating a block select as metadata to a cache line to control alignment of cache blocks in an array of the cache memory; and creating a subrow select as metadata to the cache line to control placement of at least a portion of the cache line on a particular row in the array. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the method comprises: receiving the block select and the subrow select to a controller in a processing in memory (PEVI) device, the array is a dynamic random access memory (DRAM) array, wherein the PEVI device comprises: 

 sensing circuitry coupled to the array, the sensing circuitry including a sense amplifier and a compute component configured to implement logical operations; storing the cache block in the array; and retrieving at least a portion of the cache line to perform logical operations with the compute component. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein storing the cache block in the array comprises storing a portion of a cache block having a bit length that is less than a bit length of the cache line.
